instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent at 90 degrees (tabletop position). Lift head, neck and shoulders off the mat. Extend arms alongside the body hovering a few inches off the floor. Pump the arms vigorously up and down in small controlled movements. Legs may be extended to 45 degrees for more challenge.
breathing: Inhale for 5 arm pumps, exhale for 5 arm pumps. Repeat 10 times for 100 total pumps.
modifications: Keep knees bent for beginners. Extend legs to 45 degrees for intermediate. Lower legs closer to the floor for advanced.

instructions: Lie on your back with arms extended overhead and legs straight. Inhale and begin to curl the chin toward the chest. Exhale and peel the spine off the mat one vertebra at a time, reaching the arms toward the toes. Inhale at the top. Exhale and slowly roll back down with control, articulating through every vertebra.
breathing: Inhale to prepare and initiate, exhale to roll up. Inhale at top, exhale to roll down.
modifications: Bend the knees or use a strap around the feet for beginners. Perform with straight legs and slow tempo for advanced.

instructions: Lie on your back with one leg extended toward the ceiling and the other leg flat on the mat. Arms alongside the body pressing into the floor. Circle the raised leg across the body, down, out and back up in a controlled circular motion. Keep the pelvis stable — the circle should be small enough that the hips do not rock. Reverse direction after 5 circles. Switch legs.
breathing: Inhale on the first half of the circle, exhale on the second half.
modifications: Bend the bottom leg with foot flat on the floor for stability. Make smaller circles if the pelvis rocks.

instructions: Sit at the front of the mat with knees drawn to the chest. Hold the shins with both hands. Lift the feet off the floor and balance on the tailbone. Tuck chin toward the chest. Inhale and roll backward to the shoulder blades — never onto the neck. Exhale and roll back up to the balanced starting position. Maintain the tucked ball shape throughout.
breathing: Inhale to roll back, exhale to roll up.
modifications: Hold behind the thighs instead of shins if balance is difficult. Keep feet lightly touching the mat to start.

instructions: Lie on your back with head and shoulders lifted off the mat. Draw the right knee to the chest, placing the right hand on the right ankle and the left hand on the right knee. Extend the left leg out at a 45-degree angle. Switch legs in a smooth scissoring motion, changing hand positions accordingly. Keep the core engaged and the lower back pressed into the mat.
breathing: Inhale for two leg switches, exhale for two leg switches.
modifications: Extend legs higher toward the ceiling for beginners. Lower legs closer to the floor for more challenge.

instructions: Lie on your back with head and shoulders lifted. Hug both knees to the chest, hands on shins. Inhale and simultaneously extend both arms overhead and both legs out to 45 degrees. Exhale and circle the arms around while drawing knees back to the chest. Keep the lower back anchored to the mat throughout. The movement should be smooth and coordinated.
breathing: Inhale to extend out, exhale to draw back in.
modifications: Extend legs higher for less intensity. Keep head on the mat if neck fatigues.

instructions: Sit tall with legs extended straight and slightly wider than shoulder-width, feet flexed. Extend arms forward at shoulder height. Inhale to lengthen the spine. Exhale and round forward from the crown of the head, articulating through each vertebra as though curling over an imaginary ball. Reach the fingertips past the toes. Inhale and re-stack the spine to the upright position.
breathing: Inhale to sit tall, exhale to curl forward, inhale to roll back up.
modifications: Sit on a small cushion if hamstrings are tight. Bend the knees slightly if needed.

instructions: Sit balanced on the tailbone, holding the ankles with legs extended in a wide V-shape. Find a balanced position. Inhale and roll back to the shoulder blades, keeping the open V-shape. Exhale and roll back up to the balanced position. Maintain leg extension and control throughout. Do not roll onto the neck.
breathing: Inhale to roll back, exhale to roll up.
modifications: Hold behind the knees with legs slightly bent. Straighten fully only when balance and flexibility allow.

instructions: Lie on your back with legs extended straight up toward the ceiling and arms pressed into the mat alongside the body. Keeping legs together, circle them to the right, down, to the left and back to center. The circle should be controlled and the pelvis should stay as stable as possible. Reverse the direction. Make circles smaller if the hips lift.
breathing: Inhale for half the circle, exhale for the other half.
modifications: Bend the knees to reduce lever length. Make very small circles to start.

instructions: Sit tall with legs extended wider than shoulder-width, feet flexed. Extend arms out to the sides at shoulder height. Inhale and rotate the torso to the right. Exhale and round forward, reaching the left hand past the right foot in a sawing motion. The right arm extends behind. Inhale and roll back up to the rotated position. Exhale and return to center. Repeat on the other side.
breathing: Inhale to rotate, exhale to reach forward, inhale to re-stack, exhale to center.
modifications: Sit on a cushion. Reduce the rotation range if the spine is stiff.

instructions: Lie face down with hands under the shoulders. Press up into a back extension. Release the hands and rock forward onto the chest while the legs lift. Rock back, arms extend forward, legs lower. Continue rocking like a seesaw, using momentum from the back extensors. Keep the body rigid during the rocking motion. Maintain the extension curve throughout.
breathing: Inhale as the chest lifts, exhale as the legs lift.
modifications: Practice the Swan Prep first — just the extension without releasing the hands. Build strength before attempting the dive.

instructions: Lie face down propped on the forearms with elbows directly under the shoulders. Make fists or press palms flat. Lift the chest. Bend the right knee and pulse the heel twice toward the buttock with a brisk kick-kick motion. Straighten the leg and switch to the left. Keep the hips pressed into the mat. Maintain a lifted chest throughout.
breathing: Exhale with each double kick, inhale between switches.
modifications: Rest the forehead on the hands if the back extension is too demanding.

instructions: Lie face down with the head turned to one side. Clasp the hands behind the back as high as possible. Bend both knees and pulse the heels three times toward the buttocks. Then straighten the legs, extend the clasped hands toward the feet, and lift the chest in a back extension. Lower and turn the head to the other side. Repeat.
breathing: Exhale with the three kicks, inhale as you extend and lift.
modifications: Hold a strap between the hands if clasping is difficult. Reduce range of extension.

instructions: Lie on your back with hands clasped behind the head, legs extended and hip-width apart, feet flexed. Curl up through the spine, leading with the crown of the head. Continue forward and round over the legs. Roll up to a tall seated position. Hinge back with a flat back as far as control allows, then curl the rest of the way down one vertebra at a time.
breathing: Exhale to curl up and round over. Inhale to sit tall. Exhale to hinge back and roll down.
modifications: Anchor feet under a strap or piece of furniture. Use a Roll Up approach if the full Neck Pull is too demanding.

instructions: Lie on your back and roll up into a shoulder stand supported by the hands on the lower back. Extend both legs straight up. Split the legs — one reaches toward the face, the other toward the floor. Pulse the top leg twice then switch in a scissoring motion. Keep the pelvis lifted and stable with the support of the hands. Legs stay straight throughout.
breathing: Inhale for one switch, exhale for the next.
modifications: Keep the hips on the mat and perform the scissors lying flat with the head and shoulders lifted.

instructions: From the same shoulder stand position as Scissors, bend one knee toward the chest while the other leg reaches long toward the floor. Pedal the legs as though riding a bicycle — one leg bends while the other extends. Keep the pelvis high and supported. Reverse the pedaling direction after the prescribed reps.
breathing: Breathe naturally and rhythmically with the pedaling motion.
modifications: Perform lying flat on the back with hips on the mat for a less demanding version.

instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor hip-width apart. Press hips up into a bridge. Place hands on the lower back for support. Extend the right leg straight up toward the ceiling. Lower the right leg toward the floor to hip height, then lift it back up. Repeat for the prescribed reps, then switch legs. Keep the hips level throughout.
breathing: Inhale as the leg lowers, exhale as it lifts.
modifications: Keep both feet on the floor and simply practice the bridge hold without leg extension.

instructions: Lie on your side with the body in a straight line. Prop the head on the bottom hand. Place the top hand on the floor in front of the chest for balance. Lift the top leg to hip height. Swing the leg forward with a double pulse, then swing it back past the bottom leg. Keep the torso perfectly still — only the leg moves. The pelvis should not rock.
breathing: Inhale as the leg swings forward, exhale as it swings back.
modifications: Reduce the range of the swing. Support the head on a pillow if neck strains.

instructions: Lie on your back with legs extended at a 45-degree angle and arms overhead. Inhale to prepare. Exhale and simultaneously roll the torso up and reach the arms toward the toes, arriving in a V-sit balance on the tailbone. Arms reach parallel to the legs. Inhale at the top. Exhale and roll back down with control, keeping the legs at 45 degrees. Do not let the legs drop.
breathing: Exhale to roll up, inhale to hold, exhale to roll down.
modifications: Bend the knees. Perform Teaser I with feet on the floor first, then progress to Teaser II and III.

instructions: Lie face down with arms extended forward and legs extended back. Lift the arms, chest and legs off the mat simultaneously. Flutter the arms and legs in a swimming motion — right arm and left leg lift higher while left arm and right leg lower, then alternate. Keep the torso lifted and stable. The movement is brisk but controlled.
breathing: Inhale for 5 counts of swimming, exhale for 5 counts.
modifications: Keep the range of flutter small. Lift only arms or only legs if the full movement is too challenging.

instructions: Start in a plank position with hands directly under the shoulders. Without shifting the hips, lift the right leg several inches off the mat. Pulse the heel toward the ceiling twice. Lower the foot and switch legs. Keep the body in one firm line. Do not let the hips sag or pike.
breathing: Exhale to lift the leg, inhale to lower.
modifications: Hold the plank without lifting a leg until the position is stable.

instructions: Sit with legs extended, hands behind the hips with fingers pointing toward the feet. Press into the hands and lift the hips into a reverse plank — body in one straight line from head to heels. Lift the right leg toward the ceiling without letting the hips drop. Lower the leg and switch sides. Keep the chest open and hips high throughout.
breathing: Inhale to lift the leg, exhale to lower.
modifications: Keep both feet on the floor and practice holding the reverse plank.

instructions: Sit balanced on the tailbone with knees open and feet together. Thread the arms inside the legs and wrap the hands around the outsides of the ankles. Clap the soles of the feet together three times like a seal. Roll back to the shoulder blades, clap three times, then roll back up and clap three times. Maintain the compact shape throughout.
breathing: Inhale to roll back, exhale to roll up.
modifications: Skip the clapping at first and focus on the rolling motion with control.

instructions: Sit cross-legged and hold the tops of the feet with the opposite hands (right hand holds left foot, left hand holds right foot). Round the spine and balance on the tailbone. Roll back to the shoulder blades. Roll back up past the balanced position, placing the crown of the head lightly on the mat in front. Switch the cross of the legs and repeat.
breathing: Inhale to roll back, exhale to roll up and place the head.
modifications: Skip the head placement and simply roll up to the balanced position. Cross the legs conventionally if opposite-hand grip is too difficult.

instructions: Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips. Extend the right arm forward and the left leg back simultaneously, creating a straight line from fingertips to toes. Hold for a breath. Return to the starting position and switch sides. Keep the hips and shoulders level throughout — do not rotate the torso.
breathing: Exhale to extend, inhale to return.
modifications: Extend only the arm or only the leg until stability improves.

instructions: Lie on your back with arms extended toward the ceiling and knees bent at 90 degrees in tabletop position. Slowly extend the right arm overhead and the left leg out straight, lowering both toward the floor without touching it. Return to the starting position and switch sides. Keep the lower back pressed firmly into the mat throughout the entire movement.
breathing: Exhale as you extend the arm and leg, inhale as you return.
modifications: Reduce the range — do not lower the arm and leg as far. Move only the legs while keeping arms still.

instructions: Lie on your side with knees bent at 45 degrees, heels together. Rest the head on the bottom arm. Place the top hand on the hip. Keeping the heels together and the pelvis stable, open the top knee as far as possible without rolling the hips backward. Slowly close the knee. The motion resembles a clamshell opening and closing.
breathing: Exhale to open, inhale to close.
modifications: Place a resistance band above the knees for added challenge. Reduce the range of opening if the hip rotates.

instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent at 90 degrees in tabletop position, arms alongside the body. Slowly lower the right foot toward the floor, tapping the toe lightly, then return to tabletop. Repeat with the left foot. Keep the lower back pressed into the mat and the 90-degree knee angle consistent throughout. The movement is slow and controlled.
breathing: Exhale as the toe lowers, inhale as it returns.
modifications: Tap both feet simultaneously for more challenge. Place hands under the lower back for tactile feedback.

instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor hip-width apart, arms alongside the body. Exhale and tilt the pelvis, imprinting the lower back into the mat. Continue peeling the spine off the mat one vertebra at a time — tailbone, sacrum, lower back, middle back — until the hips are lifted and the body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. Inhale at the top. Exhale and roll back down starting from the upper back, one vertebra at a time.
breathing: Exhale to curl up, inhale at the top, exhale to roll down.
modifications: Place a small ball between the knees to engage the adductors. Stop at a lower height if the back is uncomfortable.

instructions: Sit with both legs folded to the left side (Z-sit). Extend the left arm out and place the hand on the floor. Reach the right arm overhead and lean to the left, creating a long side stretch along the right side of the body. Return to center and switch — right hand on the floor, left arm reaching over to the right. Switch leg positions and repeat on the other side.
breathing: Inhale to reach, exhale to deepen the stretch, inhale to return.
modifications: Sit cross-legged if the Z-sit is uncomfortable. Use a smaller range of stretch.
